title: Koeleria barbinodis
taxonomy: Koeleria barbinodis is classified within the family Poaceae. This grass species belongs to the genus Koeleria, which is a group of cool-season grasses. Koeleria barbinodis further falls within the tribe Festuceae, which is a subfamily within the Poaceae family. The kingdom and division of this species are Plantae and Poales, respectively. It is also known by its synonyms, such as Koeleria gracilis and Koeleria tenerifolia.

The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. intro: Koeleria barbinodis, also known as the bearded hair grass, is a perennial grass species that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, Koeleria barbinodis has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Koeleria barbinodis is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions.
